content:
Hi all, I am writing bindings for virtio and V4L2.

In my latest submission I noted that it was hard to rebase on top of rust-next because quite a few things were missing. Notably, I am missing LockClassKey and LockIniter, I wonder if these will get picked soon?
Or if I could otherwise base my work on https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux instead?

Parts of the sync crate are being upstreamed now: https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/20230405175111.5974-1-wedsonaf@gmail.com/

They depend on y86's pin-init API: https://lore.kernel.org/lkml/20230405193445.745024-1-y86-dev@protonmail.com/

Where you should base your changes depends partially on when you'd like to upstream them: if soon, you should probably target rust-next. If you tell us what dependencies you have that are missing, we could take this into consideration when prioritising what is upstreamed first.

If you're not in a hurry, it's probably ok to work off of rust and wait for dependencies to make it to rust-next. We should note, however, that we are taking this opportunity to fix some issues, reconsider names, etc. So when we upstream things, they may not be exactly what we currently have in rust, so you may need to update your code.
Hi Wedson! This series will probably trigger a lengthy discussion with the other v4l2 people, so not in a hurry at all. I will rebase on top of rust for now then! :)
Please also see the notes at https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing#the-rust-subsystem (in your case, the submission looked fine, but in case others may not have seen it yet).

Hey all, I've wanted for a while to start working on rust-for-linux and wanted to pick up the virtio parts. Great to see that there is work being done on it. @Daniel Almeida are you still looking into it? I haven't seen any v3 on this. I'd love to contribute. Full disclosure, I have worked with the kernel before, however with C, so I will need to catch up with rust-for-linux ecosystem.

Babis Chalios said:

Daniel Almeida are you still looking into it? I haven't seen any v3 on this. I'd love to contribute. Full disclosure, I have worked with the kernel before, however with C, so I will need to catch up with rust-for-linux ecosystem.

@Babis Chalios Hey there, I haven't worked on a v3. Feel free to work on this if you want :)
